nameMeanings.name: Rainam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian: from the feminine form of the medieval personal name Raino, of ancient Germanic origin (from ragin ‘counsel’; see Raineri ).
Italian: possibly also a habitational name from Raina (a place near Prazzo in Cuneo province).
Italian: possibly also a nickname from the dialect word raina ‘frog’ (compare Rana ).

variants.name: Rainone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore, Angelo, Nicola, Antonietta, Carmelo, Carmine, Constantino, Cosimo, Domenic.

Italian: from the oblique case of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with the element ragin ‘advice, counsel’, or from an augmentative of the medieval personal name Raino of the same origin (see Raina ).

variants.name: Raineri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Angelo, Carmelo, Ciro, Gasper, Giovanni, Luciano, Milio, Nicola, Sal, Vincenzo.
Italian (Sicily): from Raineri, a variant of Rainero, or a patronymic or plural form of Rainero, from ancient Germanic Raginhari, from ragin ‘counsel’ + hari, heri ‘army’.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Roger Rainam was christened on 3 March 1577, in Woolverstone, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom as the son of Thomas Rainam and Rachell. He had at least 1 daughter with Mary Bartholomew.
